name: Scottish Trifle
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Scalded Heavy Cream, cooled slightly
Candied Citron or Angelica, cut into bits
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat the eggs, sugar, and vanilla extract until thick and pale. Sift flour and baking powder over egg mixture and fold them in gently but thoroughly.
2. Line the bottom of a 13x9x2-inch baking pan with parchment paper, spread batter in pan, and bake at preheated 400°F for 8 minutes or until puffed and golden. Invert onto lightly sugared kitchen towel.
3. Melt jam over low heat in a small saucepan. Stir in kirsch and spread surface of cake with jam mixture. Roll up the cake jelly-roll fashion using kitchen towel as guide, sprinkle with sherry. Wrap firmly in towel, let cool, then slice into 3/4-inch pieces.
4. In a bowl set over simmering water, beat yolks and sugar until thick and pale. Remove from heat and gradually stir in milk, cream, vanilla extract. Cook custard over simmering water until registers 170°F on candy thermometer. Transfer to metal bowl, whisk in creme fraiche and sherry. Set bowl in larger bowl of ice water, whisk until cool. Chill until cold.
5. Assemble: Scatter half amaretti crumbs in bottom of 2-1/2 qt trifle bowl. Top with half sponge roll slices, remaining crumbs on top. Sprinkle sherry over remaining slices, arrange slices along sides and pour custard, letting it seep around edges and bottom. Chill for at least 2 hours up to 24 hours.
6. Just before serving, beat cream until stiff in electric mixer bowl. Pipe rosettes around edge and center of trifle using pastry bag fitted with decorative tip. Sprinkle pistachios over trifle and garnish rosettes with citron bits and small drops of jam.
